description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a crab, featuring a broad, slightly domed carapace with a matte, sandy beige coloration. The crab has two prominent, asymmetrical claws (chelae) at the front, each with a distinct pincer shape and visible joint segmentation. Its body is flanked by four pairs of segmented walking legs on each side, all splayed outward and bent at the joints, giving the crab a lifelike, alert posture. The surface of the carapace and limbs appears hard and slightly rough, with subtle textural details suggesting a natural exoskeleton. No antennae or eyes are distinctly visible in these views, and the underside is not shown. The overall form and detailing suggest a common edible crab, suitable for depiction in urban food-related contexts.
description: A sandy beige crab with large claws and eight segmented legs, featuring a hard, textured exoskeleton and lifelike anatomical details.
description_view_0: Front view highlighting the broad carapace and both large claws extended forward, with legs partially visible to the sides.
description_view_1: Angled side view showing the curvature of the carapace, the full length of one claw, and the articulation of the legs.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of the claws and legs, and the full width of the carapace.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the other claw and the splayed arrangement of the legs.
